title: 10 Best Medicinal Preparations of Coleus forskohlii (Coleus)

preparations.constituent_desc: Coleus Forskohlii infusion is a traditional medicinal preparation made by steeping the roots or leaves of the Coleus forskohlii plant in hot water to extract its bioactive compounds. The main health benefits of this preparation include lowering blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and promoting respiratory health. The roots and leaves of the Coleus forskohlii plant are the primary plant parts used in this preparation. To make a Coleus Forskohlii infusion, add one to two teaspoons of dried and crushed Coleus forskohlii root or leaves to a cup of boiling water, let it steep for 10-15 minutes, and then strain. It is important to note that high doses of Coleus forskohlii can cause low blood pressure, so individuals with hypotension should use this preparation with caution. Additionally, pregnant or breastfeeding women and those taking medication for blood pressure or blood sugar should consult with a healthcare provider before using Coleus forskohlii infusion.

Coleus Forskohlii tincture is a medicinal preparation made by soaking the roots or leaves of the Coleus Forskohlii plant in alcohol or glycerin to extract its active compounds. The main health benefits of this tincture include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, promote weight loss, and alleviate symptoms of asthma and other respiratory conditions. The roots of the Coleus Forskohlii plant are the primary plant part used in the preparation of this tincture. To make the tincture, the roots or leaves are chopped and steeped in alcohol or glycerin for several weeks, after which the liquid is strained and stored in a cool, dark place. When using Coleus Forskohlii tincture, it is important to follow the recommended dosage and consult with a healthcare provider, as it can interact with certain medications and may have side effects such as low blood pressure, fast heart rate, and upset stomach.

Coleus Forskohlii decoction is a traditional medicinal preparation made by boiling and simmering the roots or whole plant of Coleus forskohlii in water. The main health benefits of this preparation include improved cardiovascular health, lowered blood pressure, and increased lung function. The roots and whole plant of Coleus forskohlii are the main plant parts used for this preparation. To make a Coleus Forskohlii decoction, add 1-2 teaspoons of dried and crushed Coleus forskohlii root or whole plant to a cup of water, bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer for 15-20 minutes. The main precautions to take when using Coleus forskohlii decoction include avoiding use during pregnancy and breastfeeding, and avoiding use if you have a history of low blood pressure or are taking medication for blood pressure or heart conditions. It is also recommended to consult with a healthcare provider before using Coleus forskohlii decoction if you have any pre-existing medical conditions or are taking any medications.

Coleus Forskohlii fluid extract is a concentrated, liquid herbal preparation made from the root of the Coleus forskohlii plant, a member of the mint family. The main health benefits of this preparation include lowering blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and aiding in weight loss by helping to burn body fat. The roots of the Coleus forskohlii plant are used to make the fluid extract. To make the fluid extract, the roots are first dried and then crushed or ground into a powder. The powder is then soaked in a solvent, such as alcohol or glycerin, to extract the active compounds. The final product is a concentrated liquid that contains a high amount of forskolin, the main active compound in Coleus forskohlii. When using Coleus forskohlii fluid extract, it is important to follow the recommended dosage and to consult with a healthcare provider if you have any pre-existing medical conditions or are taking any medications. Additionally, it is not recommended for use during pregnancy or while breastfeeding.

Coleus Forskohlii dry extract is a standardized preparation derived from the roots of the Coleus forskohlii plant, which has been used in traditional medicine for various health conditions. The main health benefits of this preparation include its ability to promote weight loss, lower blood pressure, and improve cardiovascular health. The roots of the Coleus forskohlii plant are used to make the dry extract, as they contain the highest concentration of active compounds. To make the dry extract, the roots are first dried and then extracted with a solvent to concentrate the active compounds. The main active compound in Coleus forskohlii is forskolin, which has been shown to have a variety of health benefits. However, it is important to note that Coleus forskohlii can interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and medications for high blood pressure, so it is important to speak with a healthcare provider before using this preparation. Additionally, pregnant or breastfeeding women should avoid using Coleus forskohlii, as its safety during pregnancy and lactation has not been established.

Coleus Forskohlii capsules are dietary supplements that contain extract from the roots of the Coleus forskohlii plant, a member of the mint family that grows in subtropical regions of India. The main health benefits of Coleus Forskohlii capsules include lowering blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, promoting weight loss, and increasing testosterone levels in men. The main plant parts used for this preparation are the roots, which contain forskolin, the primary active compound. To make Coleus Forskohlii capsules, forskolin is extracted from the roots, dried, and encapsulated. It is essential to follow the recommended dosage and consult a healthcare provider before using Coleus Forskohlii capsules, especially for individuals with low blood pressure, those taking medication for blood pressure or blood sugar, and women who are pregnant or breastfeeding.

Coleus Forskohlii tablets are dietary supplements derived from the roots of the Coleus forskohlii plant, a member of the mint family that grows in subtropical and tropical regions of the world. The main health benefits of Coleus Forskohlii tablets include promoting weight loss, lowering blood pressure, increasing testosterone levels, and improving cardiovascular health. The main plant parts used for this preparation are the roots, which contain the active compound forskolin. To make Coleus Forskohlii tablets, the roots are first dried and then ground into a fine powder, which is then compressed into tablets. It is important to follow the recommended dosage and consult with a healthcare provider before taking Coleus Forskohlii tablets, as they may interact with certain medications and are not recommended for people with certain medical conditions, such as low blood pressure or bleeding disorders.

Coleus Forskohlii softgels are dietary supplements that contain the extract of the roots of the Coleus forskohlii plant, a member of the mint family. The main health benefits of this preparation include promoting weight loss, improving cardiovascular health, and reducing inflammation. The primary plant part used for this preparation is the root of the Coleus forskohlii plant. To make this preparation, the roots of the Coleus forskohlii plant are dried and then extracted using a solvent to create a concentrated extract that is then encapsulated in a softgel form. When using Coleus forskohlii softgels, it is important to follow the recommended dosage on the product label, as taking too much can cause side effects such as low blood pressure, fast heart rate, and flushing. Additionally, people with certain medical conditions, such as ulcers, bleeding disorders, and low blood pressure, should avoid using this preparation. It is also recommended to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new supplement regimen.

Coleus Forskohlii ointment is a topical preparation made from the roots of the Coleus forskohlii plant, a member of the mint family that grows in subtropical regions of India. The main health benefits of this ointment include its potential to reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and promote wound healing due to its anti-bacterial and antioxidant properties. The primary plant part used for this preparation is the root, which contains forskolin, an active compound that has been shown to have various medicinal properties. To make the ointment, the roots are first dried and then ground into a powder, which is mixed with a suitable base such as coconut oil or shea butter. Finally, the mixture is heated and stirred until it forms a smooth, consistent ointment. When using Coleus forskohlii ointment, it is important to follow the recommended dosage and avoid applying it to broken or irritated skin. Additionally, people who are pregnant, breastfeeding, or taking medication should consult with a healthcare provider before using this ointment, as it may interact with certain drugs and have adverse effects.

Coleus Forskohlii cream is a topical preparation made from the roots of the Coleus forskohlii plant, also known as Indian Coleus or Plectranthus barbatus. The main health benefits of this preparation include its ability to potentially reduce inflammation, alleviate skin irritation, and promote wound healing. The primary plant parts used are the roots, which contain the active compound forskolin. To make Coleus forskohlii cream, dried and powdered root extract is typically combined with a carrier base such as shea butter or coconut oil. When using Coleus forskohlii cream, it is important to perform a patch test first to ensure no skin irritation occurs. Additionally, it should be kept out of the eyes and not used on broken or damaged skin. Pregnant or breastfeeding women should consult with a healthcare provider before using this or any other topical preparation.

intro: Coleus forskohlii, a member of the mint family, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential therapeutic properties. Its roots contain a compound called forskolin, which is believed to have various health benefits. A common medicinal preparation is a tincture, where the roots are soaked in alcohol to extract the active compounds. This tincture can be taken orally or added to drinks for easy consumption. Another preparation is a tisane, where the roots are steeped in hot water to make a tea. However, it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using Coleus forskohlii for medicinal purposes, as it can have interactions with other medications and may have potential side effects.
